The cars of the Washington
Water Power company will be
picketed tomorrow at Howard and
Riverside and at Natatorium park.
But few Of the members Of the
local unions know who are appoint
ed for the purpose of experimenting
any of the local union men who may
violate the laws of the unions.


1t is not probable that any great
number of members of the local
unions will think of the big park
of the local monopoly tomorrow as
there is every indication that the
nicnic at Mantle park, arranged Dy
the board of control of the Labor
World, will be attended by an i'm.
mense crowd. The new dancing
pavilion is completed and booths
and other buildings have been
built to accommodate refreshment
stands. The big zoo is more inter
esting than ever, the animals hav
ing become thoroughly tanned and
domesticated.


The Inland band will give a
splendid concert, so there is small
likelihood of the union pickets be
ing overburdened with work on &C
count Of the rush at Natatorium
park.
